limitations: The total relative-a upper estimate is below one, but not all infinite-basis assumptions are proven.
This report upgrades finite scans to explicit conditional theorem obligations, not to a completed proof.

terms.candidate_infinite_bound: |V_Hopf psi| <= b_H ||psi|| if Hopf twist coefficient is uniformly bounded or relatively D0-bounded by q^2 <= C lambda_diag
terms.finite_scaffold_bound: bounded on finite q-ranges in truncation audits
open_obligations: prove the q^2/lambda_diag comparison for the complete Berger twisted spectrum

terms.candidate_infinite_bound: |V_boundary psi| <= a_B |D0 psi| + b_B ||psi|| using Omega_f growth controlled by k,j and diagonal action
terms.finite_scaffold_bound: bounded by sector boundary residuals in finite ledgers
open_obligations: prove Omega_f growth is relatively bounded by the complete diagonal operator
terms.proof_method: relative growth bound against diagonal action

terms.candidate_infinite_bound: ||V_chi psi|| <= b_chi ||psi|| because chirality is a two-valued bounded projector
terms.finite_scaffold_bound: chirality projector is bounded in finite matrices

terms.candidate_infinite_bound: |<psi,K_sector psi>| <= a_K <psi,D0^2 psi> with a_K < 1 if the sparse/banded coupling rule is uniform in the infinite basis

title: BHSM v1.8 Uniform Relative-Bound Theorem Attempt
